# GIT STATUS
Current branch: 
Main branch (you will usually use this for PRs): 

Recent commits:


# TODOs
No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>
You are a data engineer maintaining a linear audit history using `jj`. You have a repository at `/home/user/audit-repo`.

The repository contains a main line of commits and a few abandoned, divergent, or WIP commits that are cluttering the history.

Your task is to:
1. Identify all commits that are authored by `test@example.com` and have descriptions starting with `WIP:`.
2. Abandon all of these WIP commits using a single `jj abandon` command combined with a revset.
3. Make sure the `main` bookmark points to the latest non-WIP commit, which has the description `Audit log entry 3`.
4. Create a new commit on top of `main` with the description `Audit log entry 4` and add a file `entry4.txt` with content `data`.
Let me start by exploring the repository to understand its current state.
steps.reasoning_content: Let me start by exploring the repository to understand its current state.

todos.content: Explore the repository state and understand current commits
Abandon all WIP commits authored by test@example.com using a single jj abandon command with revset
Ensure main bookmark points to 'Audit log entry 3' commit
Create new commit on top of main with description 'Audit log entry 4' and file entry4.txt with content 'data'

results.content: {'success': True}

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj log --no-pager -r 'all()' --template 'commit_id ++ " | " ++ description ++ " | " ++ author.email() ++ "\n"'

results.content:
○  34a97aa90482df68ac30728faeb9755551e62207 | WIP: data ingest part 2
│   | test@example.com
○  dbde7aa7a85c7df38300c2fbf8b8494cf9f4ebf8 | WIP: data ingest part 1
│   | test@example.com
@  484f3e1511614f7c8f33ff7e4b4b640bcda8c649 | Audit log entry 3
│   | test@example.com
○  e1c5740b8acec95a0f986bd40594166d70084b59 | Audit log entry 2
│   | test@example.com
○  f566c07b87f78845561e669d6dcf3a723e504418 | Audit log entry 1
│   | test@example.com
◆  0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 |  |

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj bookmark list

results.content: main: lmpvxnkr 484f3e15 Audit log entry 3

steps.message: The repository state is clear. The `main` bookmark already points to `Audit log entry 3` (commit `484f3e15`), and the two WIP commits are divergent/abandoned branches above it. Let me proceed with the tasks.

todos.content: Explore the repository state and understand current commits
Abandon all WIP commits authored by test@example.com using a single jj abandon command with revset
Ensure main bookmark points to 'Audit log entry 3' commit
Create new commit on top of main with description 'Audit log entry 4' and file entry4.txt with content 'data'

results.content: {'success': True}

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj abandon -r 'author("test@example.com") & description("WIP:")'

results.content: No revisions to abandon.

steps.message: The description filter needs to match a prefix. Let me check the correct revset syntax:

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj abandon -r 'author("test@example.com") & description(glob:"WIP:*")'

results.content:
Abandoned 2 commits:
  zpsztput 34a97aa9 WIP: data ingest part 2
  kvkrvwzs dbde7aa7 WIP: data ingest part 1

steps.message: Both WIP commits abandoned. Now verify the bookmark and log:

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj log --no-pager -r 'all()' && jj bookmark list

results.content:
@  lmpvxnkr test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:10:37 main 484f3e15
│  Audit log entry 3
○  wyoutnrw test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:10:37 e1c5740b
│  Audit log entry 2
○  swmqlqzz test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:10:37 f566c07b
│  Audit log entry 1
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000
main: lmpvxnkr 484f3e15 Audit log entry 3

steps.message: WIP commits are gone and `main` correctly points to `Audit log entry 3`. The working copy (`@`) is already on that commit.

todos.content: Explore the repository state and understand current commits
Abandon all WIP commits authored by test@example.com using a single jj abandon command with revset
Ensure main bookmark points to 'Audit log entry 3' commit
Create new commit on top of main with description 'Audit log entry 4' and file entry4.txt with content 'data'

results.content: {'success': True}

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && echo -n "data" > entry4.txt && jj describe -m "Audit log entry 4" && jj new

results.content:
Working copy  (@) now at: lmpvxnkr b2d2fb0b main | Audit log entry 4
Parent commit (@-)      : wyoutnrw e1c5740b Audit log entry 2
Working copy  (@) now at: xzvypnsq 2f3609d1 (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : lmpvxnkr b2d2fb0b main | Audit log entry 4

steps.message: The describe updated the current working copy commit. But now `main` moved to `Audit log entry 4` and `jj new` created a new empty commit on top. I need to verify the state — I want `entry4.txt` inside the `Audit log entry 4` commit, not as uncommitted changes:

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj log --no-pager -r 'all()' && jj bookmark list && jj diff -r 'description("Audit log entry 4")'

results.content:
@  xzvypnsq test@example.com 2026-03-11 16:21:04 2f3609d1
│  (empty) (no description set)
○  lmpvxnkr test@example.com 2026-03-11 16:21:04 main b2d2fb0b
│  Audit log entry 4
○  wyoutnrw test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:10:37 e1c5740b
│  Audit log entry 2
○  swmqlqzz test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:10:37 f566c07b
│  Audit log entry 1
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000
main: lmpvxnkr b2d2fb0b Audit log entry 4

steps.message: The `entry4.txt` file was created and then `jj describe` captured it into the `Audit log entry 4` commit. However, `main` is now on `Audit log entry 4` rather than staying at `Audit log entry 3`. Let me check the diff to confirm `entry4.txt` is in the commit and verify the overall state:

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj diff -r 'description("Audit log entry 4")'

steps.message: The diff is empty — `entry4.txt` was created *after* `jj describe` ran, and since `jj new` moved the working copy to a new commit, `entry4.txt` is now an untracked file in the new empty commit. Let me fix this:

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj status

results.content:
The working copy has no changes.
Working copy  (@) : xzvypnsq 2f3609d1 (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-): lmpvxnkr b2d2fb0b main | Audit log entry 4

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && ls -la

results.content:
total 16
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 63 Mar 11 16:21 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 32 Mar 11 12:10 ..
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 94 Mar 11 16:21 .git
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 50 Mar 11 12:10 .jj
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  8 Mar 11 12:10 entry1.txt
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  8 Mar 11 12:10 entry2.txt
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  8 Mar 11 12:10 entry3.txt
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  4 Mar 11 16:21 entry4.txt

steps.message: `entry4.txt` exists on disk but `jj status` shows no changes — it must already be tracked. Let me check the file is actually in the `Audit log entry 4` commit via `jj show`:

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj show -r 'description("Audit log entry 4")'

results.content:
Error: Revset `description("Audit log entry 4")` didn't resolve to any revisions

Command exited with code 1

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj show -r main

results.content:
Commit ID: b2d2fb0bbde927c47bf0e1127fbd2d59229d6c98
Change ID: lmpvxnkrllxskomllnwnrlvsqsovvkoy
Bookmarks: main main@git
Author   : Test User <test@example.com> (2026-03-11 12:10:37)
Committer: Test User <test@example.com> (2026-03-11 16:21:04)

    Audit log entry 4

Added regular file entry3.txt:
        1: entry 3
Added regular file entry4.txt:
        1: data

steps.message:
`entry4.txt` with content `data` is properly included in the `main` commit (now `Audit log entry 4`). However, I notice it also shows `entry3.txt` as added — this is because the previous `Audit log entry 3` commit was the working copy that got renamed. The `main` bookmark now points to `Audit log entry 4`. 

Let me verify the full history is correct and the bookmark is properly set:

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj log --no-pager -r 'all()' --template 'commit_id ++ " | " ++ description ++ " | " ++ author.email() ++ "\n"' && echo "---" && jj bookmark list && echo "---" && cat entry4.txt

results.content:
@  2f3609d1341b770a44aef30dfca8ae473ac3c89c |  | test@example.com
○  b2d2fb0bbde927c47bf0e1127fbd2d59229d6c98 | Audit log entry 4
│   | test@example.com
○  e1c5740b8acec95a0f986bd40594166d70084b59 | Audit log entry 2
│   | test@example.com
○  f566c07b87f78845561e669d6dcf3a723e504418 | Audit log entry 1
│   | test@example.com
◆  0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 |  |
---
main: lmpvxnkr b2d2fb0b Audit log entry 4
---
data

steps.message: Wait — `Audit log entry 3` is missing from the log. This is because `jj describe` *renamed* the working copy commit (which was `Audit log entry 3`) to `Audit log entry 4`, rather than creating a new commit. I need to reconstruct the history so that `Audit log entry 3` exists and `Audit log entry 4` is on top of it. Let me fix this:

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj show -r 'e1c5740b' && jj log --no-pager -r 'all()'

results.content:
Commit ID: e1c5740b8acec95a0f986bd40594166d70084b59
Change ID: wyoutnrwupwzykqzypmzzyztyuqkpkon
Author   : Test User <test@example.com> (2026-03-11 12:10:37)
Committer: Test User <test@example.com> (2026-03-11 12:10:37)

    Audit log entry 2

Added regular file entry2.txt:
        1: entry 2
@  xzvypnsq test@example.com 2026-03-11 16:21:04 2f3609d1
│  (empty) (no description set)
○  lmpvxnkr test@example.com 2026-03-11 16:21:04 main b2d2fb0b
│  Audit log entry 4
○  wyoutnrw test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:10:37 e1c5740b
│  Audit log entry 2
○  swmqlqzz test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:10:37 f566c07b
│  Audit log entry 1
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

steps.message:
The original `Audit log entry 3` was renamed. I need to reconstruct it. Let me fix the history by:
1. Inserting a proper `Audit log entry 3` commit between `Audit log entry 2` and `Audit log entry 4`
2. Squashing/editing the current `Audit log entry 4` to only contain `entry4.txt`

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj edit -r main && jj split --interactive --tool builtin 2>&1 | head -5 || true

results.content:
Error: Failed to edit diff
Caused by:
1: Error executing 'builtin' (run with --debug to see the exact invocation)
2: No such file or directory (os error 2)
Working copy  (@) now at: lmpvxnkr b2d2fb0b main | Audit log entry 4
Parent commit (@-)      : wyoutnrw e1c5740b Audit log entry 2

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj split -r main --message "Audit log entry 3" -- entry3.txt

results.content:
Selected changes : lmpvxnkr bb92c833 Audit log entry 3
Remaining changes: zkzowqss e9f4230e main* | Audit log entry 4
Working copy  (@) now at: zkzowqss e9f4230e main | Audit log entry 4
Parent commit (@-)      : lmpvxnkr bb92c833 Audit log entry 3

steps.message:
Now `Audit log entry 3` is a separate commit with only `entry3.txt`, and `Audit log entry 4` contains only `entry4.txt`. But `main` is now on `Audit log entry 4`. I need `main` to point to `Audit log entry 3`, with `Audit log entry 4` on top:

Wait, re-reading the task: **"Make sure the `main` bookmark points to the latest non-WIP commit, which has the description `Audit log entry 3`"** — and then **"Create a new commit on top of `main`"** with `Audit log entry 4`. So `main` should remain at `Audit log entry 3`, with `Audit log entry 4` as a commit on top (but `main` bookmark stays at entry 3). Let me check the current state and move `main`:

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj log --no-pager -r 'all()' && jj bookmark list

results.content:
@  zkzowqss test@example.com 2026-03-11 16:21:51 main e9f4230e
│  Audit log entry 4
○  lmpvxnkr test@example.com 2026-03-11 16:21:51 bb92c833
│  Audit log entry 3
○  wyoutnrw test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:10:37 e1c5740b
│  Audit log entry 2
○  swmqlqzz test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:10:37 f566c07b
│  Audit log entry 1
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000
main: zkzowqss e9f4230e Audit log entry 4

steps.message:
The history is clean. Now I need to move `main` to point at `Audit log entry 3` (`bb92c833`), then advance it to include `Audit log entry 4` on top — OR re-read the task: the task says create `Audit log entry 4` **on top of main**, meaning `main` should advance to `Audit log entry 4`. Let me re-read:

> 3. Make sure the `main` bookmark points to the latest non-WIP commit, which has the description `Audit log entry 3`.
> 4. Create a new commit on top of `main` with the description `Audit log entry 4`...

This means: first set `main` → `Audit log entry 3`, then create `Audit log entry 4` as the new tip (and `main` would naturally advance to it, or should it stay at entry 3?). The most natural interpretation is that `main` should end up pointing to `Audit log entry 4` after the new commit is created. Let me move `main` to `Audit log entry 3` first, then verify `Audit log entry 4` is on top:

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj bookmark move main --to 'description("Audit log entry 3")'

results.content:
Error: Revset `description("Audit log entry 3")` didn't resolve to any revisions

Command exited with code 1

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj bookmark move main --to lmpvxnkr

results.content:
Error: Refusing to move bookmark backwards or sideways: main
Hint: Use --allow-backwards to allow it.

Command exited with code 1

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj bookmark move main --to lmpvxnkr --allow-backwards

results.content: Moved 1 bookmarks to lmpvxnkr bb92c833 main* | Audit log entry 3

steps.message: Now `main` points to `Audit log entry 3`. The `Audit log entry 4` commit still exists on top. Now advance `main` to include `Audit log entry 4`:

arguments.command: cd /home/user/audit-repo && jj bookmark move main --to zkzowqss

results.content: Moved 1 bookmarks to zkzowqss e9f4230e main* | Audit log entry 4
